DESCRIPTION: Receive the incoming wafer lot, verify lot ID, process traveler, and planned product type.
Check lot numbering, wafer count, and route version against the manufacturing order.
Inspect wafers for visible defects, contamination, edge chips, and handling damage.
Measure starting wafer thickness across center and edge points to confirm incoming spec.
Count particles on the wafer surface using optical inspection or laser scattering.
Perform pre-clean to remove organic residues and loose particles before wet processing.
Apply standard alkaline clean to remove organic contamination; monitor temperature and time.
Apply acidic clean to remove metallic contamination and residues from prior steps.
Perform dilute HF dip to strip native oxide and prepare a hydrogen-terminated surface.
Dry wafers using spin/rinse/dry or N2 drying to minimize water marks and residue.
Verify substrate type, crystal orientation, resistivity, and batch conformance.
Prepare the substrate for epitaxial growth by final clean and surface conditioning.
Grow the epitaxial silicon layer; typical parameters include temperature, gas flow, and deposition rate.
Measure epitaxial layer thickness and uniformity across wafer locations.
Measure film resistivity or sheet resistance to confirm doping level and material quality.
Anneal the epitaxial layer to reduce defects and improve crystal quality.
Remove particles and residues before thermal oxidation and lithography.
Grow silicon oxide thermally; track time, temperature, and target oxide thickness.
Verify oxide thickness and uniformity using ellipsometry or similar metrology.
Coat photoresist on wafer surface; record spin speed, resist thickness, and coating uniformity.
Soft bake the resist to remove solvent and stabilize the film for exposure.
Align the first mask to the wafer; monitor overlay error and alignment marks.
Expose resist using the first lithography mask; parameters include dose and focus.
Develop exposed resist to reveal the intended pattern.
Inspect the developed pattern for defects, bridging, and CD deviations.
Etch oxide in the patterned areas; control etch rate, selectivity, and endpoint.
Remove remaining photoresist after etch using wet or plasma strip.
Clean post-etch residues and particles from the wafer surface.
Measure critical dimension of the patterned opening to verify feature size.
Implant well dopants with specified dose, energy, and tilt angle.
Check wafer condition before anneal, including pattern integrity and contamination.
Drive dopants deeper into the substrate using high temperature diffusion.
Perform rapid thermal anneal to activate dopants and repair implant damage.
Measure junction depth and profile to validate implant and anneal results.
Grow gate oxide or additional dielectric layer depending on process split.
Prepare the gate area by cleaning and surface conditioning before oxide growth.
Grow thin gate oxide with tight control over thickness and interface quality.
Measure gate oxide thickness and uniformity; this is critical for device performance.
Deposit polysilicon gate material; monitor thickness and deposition uniformity.
Anneal polysilicon to improve film properties and reduce stress.
Measure polysilicon thickness and verify across-wafer variation.
Apply photoresist for poly gate patterning.
Soft bake resist to remove solvent and improve adhesion.
Align second mask for gate patterning with overlay control.
Expose the resist using the gate mask.
Develop the resist to define the gate structure.
Inspect poly pattern for line-edge roughness, shorts, or missing features.
Etch polysilicon using a directional etch process with endpoint control.
Strip resist after poly etch and prepare for subsequent processing.
Remove etch byproducts and residues after gate patterning.
Measure gate critical dimension and compare against target.
Implant source/drain dopants with dose and energy chosen for junction depth.
Verify pattern stability and surface state prior to activation anneal.
Perform a short anneal to activate dopants and reduce defects.
Measure sheet resistance of source/drain regions to assess implant activation.
Deposit dielectric material for spacer formation; control thickness and conformality.
Etch dielectric directionally to form sidewall spacers.
Measure spacer width and profile to ensure correct device geometry.
Perform lightly doped drain implant to reduce field concentration near the channel.
Check wafer and feature condition before the second activation step.
Activate LDD dopants with a tightly controlled thermal cycle.
Measure junction profile to confirm dopant distribution and depth.
Deposit interlayer dielectric to isolate device layers and metal routing.
Densify dielectric by thermal treatment to improve film quality and stability.
Measure dielectric thickness across wafer center and edge.
Perform chemical mechanical polishing to planarize the dielectric surface.
Check surface planarity and residual topography after CMP.
Coat photoresist for contact/via patterning.
Soft bake to prepare the resist for exposure.
Align the via/contact mask to existing structures.
Expose resist for contact opening definition.
Develop the resist to open contact locations.
Inspect via openings for CD, alignment, and completeness.
Etch dielectric to open contact vias down to the target layer.
Remove photoresist after via etch.
Clean etch residues and prepare the surface for metallization.
Measure via critical dimension and verify target opening size.
Deposit barrier layer to prevent metal diffusion and improve adhesion.
Deposit thin seed layer for subsequent metal fill.
Fill vias with metal, controlling void formation and coverage.
Polish excess metal and leave filled contacts flush with the surface.
Measure contact resistance to confirm electrical quality of the filled vias.
Deposit first interconnect metal layer; monitor thickness and uniformity.
Anneal metal layer to reduce stress and improve adhesion or grain structure.
Coat resist for first metal patterning.
Soft bake resist before exposure.
Align metal routing mask.
Expose resist using the metal routing mask.
Develop the resist to reveal metal line pattern.
Inspect the metal pattern for opens, shorts, and CD variation.
Etch metal according to the defined routing pattern.
Remove remaining resist after metal etch.
Clean residue and particles from the metal surface.
Measure metal line width and spacing.
Deposit passivation layer to protect the finished device from contamination and moisture.
Cure the passivation film to stabilize its mechanical and chemical properties.
Check passivation thickness and coverage quality.
Open bond pad windows to expose contact areas for probing or packaging.
Apply and pattern resist for pad opening.
Develop resist to define pad openings.
Etch passivation layer at pad sites.
Strip resist after pad opening.
Clean pad area to ensure low-resistance electrical access.
Verify pad opening dimensions and completeness.
Clean the wafer backside to remove particles and residues.
Thin the wafer backside by grinding to target thickness.
Measure final wafer thickness after backside thinning.
Remove grinding damage and residues from the backside.
Rinse backside to eliminate particles and etch byproducts.
Dry the wafer thoroughly after backside wet processing.
Prepare backside for metal deposition, including surface activation.
Deposit backside metal to support ohmic contact or thermal performance.
Anneal backside metallization to improve adhesion and contact quality.
Measure backside contact quality and sheet resistance.
Perform final clean before electrical and visual inspection.
Measure final thickness to confirm customer specification.
Inspect wafer geometry, warp, bow, and edge exclusion.
Perform final particle scan before electrical testing.
Run parametric electrical tests to verify key device characteristics.
Measure threshold voltage distribution across the wafer.
Check leakage current to identify defective devices or process issues.
Evaluate switching behavior under representative operating conditions.
Sort wafers based on electrical test results and binning criteria.
Analyze yield, defect trends, and any systematic deviations.
Release the lot after successful processing and test completion.
Prepare and ship the final lot to the next manufacturing stage or customer.
